+title: Add comment-log display + editor and register them in the field registry (UI)
+---
+## What
+Surface the conversation log in the task inspector. The inspector (`apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspectors-container.tsx` ŌåÆ `EntityInspector`) renders every schema field through the metadata-driven field registry, so a new `comment-log` display/editor registered with the registry will appear automatically once the `comments` field is on the task entity (dependency task). Do NOT hardcode comment logic anywhere in inspector/EntityInspector ŌĆö register components and let the metadata drive it (per the "UI is an interpreter of Field metadata" rule).
+
+Follow the attachment field UI precedent exactly:
+- `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/registrations/attachment.tsx` (registration adapters)
+- `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/displays/attachment-display.tsx`
+- `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/editors/attachment-editor.tsx`
+
+Files:
+1. `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/displays/comment-log-display.tsx` ŌĆö renders the comments array as a chronological thread: each member shows the actor (resolve actor id ŌåÆ name/avatar the same way assignees/avatar display does), the text, and a relative/absolute timestamp (reuse the existing date-display formatting). Read-only display.
+2. `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/editors/comment-log-editor.tsx` ŌĆö a compose box to add a new comment (text input + submit) and, optionally, edit/delete affordances on existing members. Submitting dispatches the `add comment` command via `useDispatchCommand` (see how attachment-editor / other editors dispatch). Author defaults to the current actor on the backend; the editor does not need to pass actor.
+3. `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/registrations/comment-log.tsx` ŌĆö `registerDisplay("comment-log", ...)` and `registerEditor("comment-log", ...)` adapters.
+4. `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/registrations/index.ts` ŌĆö add `import "./comment-log";` (this is the central registration index; a new file MUST be imported here or it never registers).
+5. Add the `comment-log` editor/display names to the TS `FieldType`/type plumbing if the UI maintains an enum mirror (`apps/kanban-app/ui/src/types/kanban.ts` has a `FieldType` ŌĆö check whether display/editor names are typed there; the Rust side treats them as free strings, mirror that).
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] Opening a task inspector renders the `comments` field via the `comment-log` display, showing each member's actor, text, and timestamp.
+- [ ] The editor can add a new comment; submitting dispatches `add comment` and the new member appears (reactively, via the field-change event from the events task).
+- [ ] No comment-specific branching exists in `EntityInspector`/inspector code ŌĆö all behavior flows through the registry.
+- [ ] `npm run lint` and `npx tsc --noEmit` clean in `apps/kanban-app/ui`.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] `comment-log-display.test.tsx` (modeled on `attachment-display.test.tsx`): given a `comments` value with two members, asserts both texts, both resolved actor labels, and timestamps render in order.
+- [ ] `comment-log-editor.test.tsx` (modeled on `attachment-editor.test.tsx`): typing text + submit dispatches the `add comment` command with the right args; empty submit is a no-op.
+- [ ] A browser/integration test (modeled on `board-integration.browser.test.tsx`, which already references a `comments` field shape) drives the inspector and asserts the comment thread renders from field metadata.
+- [ ] `npm test` in `apps/kanban-app/ui` ŌĆö green.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` ŌĆö write the display + editor component tests first, then implement the components and register them.
\ No newline at end of file

Same shape for a pending `{text}` add (the typed comment vanishes at resync and is dropped if a follow-up op lands in the window). Suggest rebasing instead of wholesale reset: on `value` change, re-apply local un-acknowledged tombstones whose ids still exist in the fresh server value (tombstones are idempotent server-side) and keep pending `{text}` members until a server round-trip mints them; or alternatively flush the pending save before accepting an external resync. If you instead decide the 1s window is acceptable, document the accepted race in the draft-resync comment so the next reader doesn't rediscover it.

+2. **Resync race** ŌĆö replaced the wholesale draft reset with `rebaseComments(draft, baseline, fresh)` (new pure helper in `comment-utils.ts`; the editor tracks the last-rebased server value in `baselineRef`).
